title: tordylium byzantinum
intro: Tordylium byzantinum, also known as wild heliotrope or meadow heliotrope, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and aromatic foliage. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Tordylium byzantinum has been valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and promote the healing of wounds. Tordylium byzantinum can be prepared as an herbal infusion, tincture, or decoction, which can be used to address various health concerns and support overall well-being.
